4. DATA TYPE
   .f16 or .bf16 (both supported as indicated in the caption)

5. GROUPING PATTERNS
   Threads are grouped in blocks of 4 consecutive threads per row-pair group:
     T0-T3:   cover row pairs 0-1, 2-3, 4-5, 6-7 respectively, all at column 0
     T4-T7:   cover row pairs 0-1, 2-3, 4-5, 6-7 respectively, at column 1
     T8-T11:  column 2 (implied by arrow continuation)
     T12-T15: column 3
     T16-T19: column 4
     T20-T23: column 5
     T24-T27: column 6
     T28-T31: cover row pairs 0-1, 2-3, 4-5, 6-7 respectively, at column 7

   General rule: Thread T(n) covers:
     Row pair = n mod 4 (i.e., row pair index 0,1,2,3 corresponding to rows 0-1, 2-3, 4-5, 6-7)
     Column = floor(n / 4)

6. REGISTER PACKING
   Each thread holds exactly 2 register elements: b0 and b1
   These are packed as two f16 (or bf16) values within a single 32-bit register
   b0 occupies the lower 16 bits of the register
   b1 occupies the upper 16 bits of the register
   Each thread thus holds one 32-bit register containing two consecutive elements from its assigned matrix position

   The notation %laneid:{fragments} at the bottom confirms the mapping is indexed by lane ID within the warp.

    >
    >
    > Figure 74 MMA .m16n8k8 fragment layout for matrix B with `.f16` / `.bf16` type.
    >
    > The row and column of a matrix fragment can be computed as:
    >
    > ```
    > groupID           = %laneid >> 2
    > threadID_in_group = %laneid % 4
    >
    > row = (threadID_in_group * 2) + i       for bi    where i = {0, 1}
    >
    > col =  groupID
    > ```
  + `.tf32` :

    > | .btype | Fragment | Elements (low to high) |
    > | --- | --- | --- |
    > | `.tf32` | A vector expression containing two `.b32` registers, containing two `.tf32` elements from the matrix B. | b0, b1 |
    >
    > The layout of the fragments held by different threads is shown in [Figure 75](#mma-1688-b-tf32).

MMA .m16n8k8 Fragment Layout for Matrix B with .f64 Type

1. MATRIX DIMENSIONS AND IDENTITY
   Instruction: MMA .m16n8k8
   Matrix: B (input matrix)
   Data type: .f64 (64-bit double precision floating point)
   Matrix B logical dimensions: K x N = 8 rows x 8 columns

2. GRID STRUCTURE
   The table has:
   Rows (labeled 0 through 7): represent the K dimension (8 rows of matrix B)
   Columns (labeled 0, 1, 2, .., 7): represent the N dimension (8 columns of matrix B)
   The table is divided into two horizontal sections:
     Upper section: rows 0-3 (K rows 0 through 3)
     Lower section: rows 4-7 (K rows 4 through 7)

3. ELEMENT-TO-THREAD-TO-REGISTER MAPPING

   Upper section (rows 0-3), register b0:
     Row 0, Column 0: T0:b0
     Row 0, Column 1: T4:b0
     Row 0, Column 7: T28:b0
     Row 1, Column 0: T1:b0
     Row 1, Column 1: T5:b0
     Row 1, Column 7: T29:b0
     Row 2, Column 0: T2:b0
     Row 2, Column 1: T6:b0
     Row 2, Column 7: T30:b0
     Row 3, Column 0: T3:b0
     Row 3, Column 1: T7:b0
     Row 3, Column 7: T31:b0

   Lower section (rows 4-7), register b1:
     Row 4, Column 0: T0:b1
     Row 4, Column 1: T4:b1
     Row 4, Column 7: T28:b1
     Row 5, Column 0: T1:b1
     Row 5, Column 1: T5:b1
     Row 5, Column 7: T29:b1
     Row 6, Column 0: T2:b1
     Row 6, Column 1: T6:b1
     Row 6, Column 7: T30:b1
     Row 7, Column 0: T3:b1
     Row 7, Column 1: T7:b1
     Row 7, Column 7: T31:b1

   Columns 2 through 6 (indicated by ".." with diagonal arrows) follow the same pattern as shown for columns 0, 1, and 7, continuing the thread numbering pattern.

4. THREAD GROUPING PATTERN
   Threads are grouped in sets of 4 consecutive threads per column group:
     Column 0: T0, T1, T2, T3 (rows 0-3 for b0; rows 4-7 for b1)
     Column 1: T4, T5, T6, T7 (rows 0-3 for b0; rows 4-7 for b1)
     Column 2: T8, T9, T10, T11 (implied by pattern)
     Column 3: T12, T13, T14, T15 (implied)
     Column 4: T16, T17, T18, T19 (implied)
     Column 5: T20, T21, T22, T23 (implied)
     Column 6: T24, T25, T26, T27 (implied)
     Column 7: T28, T29, T30, T31 (rows 0-3 for b0; rows 4-7 for b1)

   Each group of 4 threads (Tn, Tn+1, Tn+2, Tn+3) covers one column of matrix B across all 8 K-rows, using registers b0 (for K-rows 0-3) and b1 (for K-rows 4-7).

5. REGISTER PACKING
   Each thread holds 2 registers for matrix B:
     b0: holds the element from K-rows 0-3 for that thread's assigned column
     b1: holds the element from K-rows 4-7 for that thread's assigned column
   Since the data type is .f64 (64-bit), each register b0 and b1 is a 64-bit register holding one double-precision value. There is no sub-register packing (one element per register).

6. NOTATION
   %laneid:{fragments} indicates that the thread lane ID determines which fragment (matrix element) each thread holds.
   The diagonal arrows in columns 2 and ".." indicate the pattern continues consistently for all intermediate columns following the same thread-numbering scheme.

1. MATRIX DIMENSIONS AND TYPE
   Instruction: MMA .m16n8k8
   Matrix: C/D (accumulator)
   Data type: .f64 (64-bit double precision floating point)
   Matrix dimensions: M=16 rows, N=8 columns, K=8

2. GRID STRUCTURE
   The table has:
   - Rows labeled 0 through 15 (matrix rows), with rows 2 through 6 compressed (indicated by arrows and ".." notation), and rows 10 through 14 compressed similarly
   - Columns labeled 0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7 (matrix columns), grouped in pairs: (0,1), (2,3), (4,5), (6,7)
   - Each cell shows a thread identifier and the register fragments it holds

3. THREAD-TO-ELEMENT MAPPING (complete explicit rows shown)

   UPPER HALF OF MATRIX (rows 0-7), register fragments c0 and c1:

   Row 0:  Cols 0-1: T0  holds {c0, c1}
           Cols 2-3: T1  holds {c0, c1}
           Cols 4-5: T2  holds {c0, c1}
           Cols 6-7: T3  holds {c0, c1}

   Row 1:  Cols 0-1: T4  holds {c0, c1}
           Cols 2-3: T5  holds {c0, c1}
           Cols 4-5: T6  holds {c0, c1}
           Cols 6-7: T7  holds {c0, c1}

   Rows 2-6: Pattern continues with threads T8 through T27 following the same structure (indicated by diagonal arrows showing continuation pattern)

   Row 7:  Cols 0-1: T28 holds {c0, c1}
           Cols 2-3: T29 holds {c0, c1}
           Cols 4-5: T30 holds {c0, c1}
           Cols 6-7: T31 holds {c0, c1}

   LOWER HALF OF MATRIX (rows 8-15), register fragments c2 and c3:

   Row 8:  Cols 0-1: T0  holds {c2, c3}
           Cols 2-3: T1  holds {c2, c3}
           Cols 4-5: T2  holds {c2, c3}
           Cols 6-7: T3  holds {c2, c3}

   Row 9:  Cols 0-1: T4  holds {c2, c3}
           Cols 2-3: T5  holds {c2, c3}
           Cols 4-5: T6  holds {c2, c3}
           Cols 6-7: T7  holds {c2, c3}

   Rows 10-14: Pattern continues with threads T8 through T27 following the same structure (indicated by diagonal arrows)

   Row 15: Cols 0-1: T28 holds {c2, c3}
           Cols 2-3: T29 holds {c2, c3}
           Cols 4-5: T30 holds {c2, c3}
           Cols 6-7: T31 holds {c2, c3}

4. THREAD GROUPING PATTERN
   - Threads T0-T31 (all 32 threads in a warp) are used
   - Each thread covers exactly 2 consecutive columns (a column pair) in one row
   - Threads T0-T3 handle row 0 (columns 0-1, 2-3, 4-5, 6-7 respectively)
   - Threads T4-T7 handle row 1
   - Each subsequent group of 4 threads handles the next row
   - Threads T28-T31 handle row 7
   - The same threads T0-T31 are reused for rows 8-15, but with different register fragments (c2, c3 instead of c0, c1)

5. REGISTER ELEMENT PACKING
   - Each .f64 element occupies one 64-bit register (or a pair of 32-bit registers in PTX)
   - Each thread holds 2 elements for the upper half (c0, c1) covering rows 0-7
   - Each thread holds 2 elements for the lower half (c2, c3) covering rows 8-15
   - Total per thread: 4 fragments total: {c0, c1, c2, c3}
   - c0 and c1 correspond to the two column positions within the thread's assigned column pair in rows 0-7
   - c2 and c3 correspond to the two column positions within the thread's assigned column pair in rows 8-15

6. LABEL AT BOTTOM
   %laneid:{fragments} — indicating the notation used: lane ID maps to the listed fragments

   UPPER HALF OF MATRIX (rows 0-7), register fragments c0 and c1:

   Row 0:  Cols 0-1: T0  holds {c0, c1}

   Row 7:  Cols 0-1: T28 holds {c0, c1}
           Cols 2-3: T29 holds {c0, c1}
           Cols 4-5: T30 holds {c0, c1}
           Cols 6-7: T31 holds {c0, c1}

   Row 8:  Cols 0-1: T0  holds {c2, c3}

           Cols 6-7: T31 holds {c2, c3}

4. THREAD GROUPING PATTERN
   - Threads T0-T31 (all 32 threads in a warp) are used
   - Each thread covers exactly 2 consecutive columns (a column pair) in one row
   - Threads T0-T3 handle row 0 (columns 0-1, 2-3, 4-5, 6-7 respectively)
   - Threads T4-T7 handle row 1
   - Each subsequent group of 4 threads handles the next row
   - Threads T28-T31 handle row 7
   - The same threads T0-T31 are reused for rows 8-15, but with different register fragments (c2, c3 instead of c0, c1)

5. REGISTER ELEMENT PACKING
   - Each .f64 element occupies one 64-bit register (or a pair of 32-bit registers in PTX)
   - Each thread holds 2 elements for the upper half (c0, c1) covering rows 0-7
   - Each thread holds 2 elements for the lower half (c2, c3) covering rows 8-15
   - Total per thread: 4 fragments total: {c0, c1, c2, c3}
   - c0 and c1 correspond to the two column positions within the thread's assigned column pair in rows 0-7
   - c2 and c3 correspond to the two column positions within the thread's assigned column pair in rows 8-15

##### 9.7.14.5.8. [Matrix Fragments for `mma.m16n8k16` with floating point type](#warp-level-matrix-fragment-mma-16816-float)

A warp executing `mma.m16n8k16` floating point types will compute an MMA operation of shape
`.m16n8k16`.

Elements of the matrix are distributed across the threads in a warp so each thread of the warp holds
a fragment of the matrix.

* Multiplicand A:

  + `.f16` and `.bf16` :

    > | .atype | Fragment | Elements (low to high) |
    > | --- | --- | --- |
    > | `.f16` / `.bf16` | A vector expression containing four `.f16x2` registers, with each register containing two `.f16` / `.bf16` elements from the matrix A. | a0, a1, a2, a3, a4, a5, a6, a7 |
    >
    > The layout of the fragments held by different threads is shown in [Figure 79](#mma-16816-a-f16).
    >
    > 



[Figure: mma-16816-A-f16]
MATRIX MMA FRAGMENT LAYOUT DESCRIPTION
=======================================

INSTRUCTION: mma .m16n8k16
MATRIX: A (input matrix)
DATA TYPE: .f16 or .bf16
DIMENSIONS: M=16, N=8, K=16

GRID INTERPRETATION
-------------------
Rows (R) = matrix rows 0 through 15 (the M=16 dimension)
Columns (C) = matrix columns 0 through 15 (the K=16 dimension)
The table is divided into two halves:
  Left half: columns 0-7
  Right half: columns 8-15

REGISTER PACKING
----------------
Two f16 (or bf16) values are packed into a single 32-bit register.
Each thread holds 4 registers total: {a0,a1}, {a2,a3}, {a4,a5}, {a6,a7}
- {a0,a1} = one 32-bit register holding two f16 values (elements a0 and a1)
- {a2,a3} = one 32-bit register holding two f16 values (elements a2 and a3)
- {a4,a5} = one 32-bit register holding two f16 values (elements a4 and a5)
- {a6,a7} = one 32-bit register holding two f16 values (elements a6 and a7)

THREAD-TO-ELEMENT MAPPING (complete table)
------------------------------------------
Format: Row, Column range -> Thread: {fragments}

ROWS 0-7, COLUMNS 0-7 (left-top quadrant):
  Row 0, Cols 0-3: T0:{a0,a1}  T1:{a0,a1}  T2:{a0,a1}  T3:{a0,a1}
  Row 1, Cols 0-3: T4:{a0,a1}  T5:{a0,a1}  T6:{a0,a1}  T7:{a0,a1}
  Rows 2-6: continuation pattern (arrows indicate same pattern repeating)
  Row 7, Cols 0-3: T28:{a0,a1} T29:{a0,a1} T30:{a0,a1} T31:{a0,a1}

ROWS 0-7, COLUMNS 8-15 (right-top quadrant):
  Row 0, Cols 8-11:  T0:{a4,a5}  T1:{a4,a5}  T2:{a4,a5}  T3:{a4,a5}
  Row 1, Cols 8-11:  T4:{a4,a5}  T5:{a4,a5}  T6:{a4,a5}  T7:{a4,a5}
  Rows 2-6: continuation pattern
  Row 7, Cols 8-11:  T28:{a4,a5} T29:{a4,a5} T30:{a4,a5} T31:{a4,a5}

ROWS 8-15, COLUMNS 0-7 (left-bottom quadrant):
  Row 8,  Cols 0-3: T0:{a2,a3}  T1:{a2,a3}  T2:{a2,a3}  T3:{a2,a3}
  Row 9,  Cols 0-3: T4:{a2,a3}  T5:{a2,a3}  T6:{a2,a3}  T7:{a2,a3}
  Rows 10-14: continuation pattern
  Row 15, Cols 0-3: T28:{a2,a3} T29:{a2,a3} T30:{a2,a3} T31:{a2,a3}

ROWS 8-15, COLUMNS 8-15 (right-bottom quadrant):
  Row 8,  Cols 8-11:  T0:{a6,a7}  T1:{a6,a7}  T2:{a6,a7}  T3:{a6,a7}
  Row 9,  Cols 8-11:  T4:{a6,a7}  T5:{a6,a7}  T6:{a6,a7}  T7:{a6,a7}
  Rows 10-14: continuation pattern
  Row 15, Cols 8-11:  T28:{a6,a7} T29:{a6,a7} T30:{a6,a7} T31:{a6,a7}

GROUPING PATTERN SUMMARY
-------------------------
Threads are grouped in sets of 4 consecutive threads, each group covering one matrix row:
  Row 0:  T0,  T1,  T2,  T3   (cols 0-3 and 8-11)
  Row 1:  T4,  T5,  T6,  T7   (cols 0-3 and 8-11)
  Row 2:  T8,  T9,  T10, T11  (cols 0-3 and 8-11)
  Row 3:  T12, T13, T14, T15  (cols 0-3 and 8-11)
  Row 4:  T16, T17, T18, T19  (cols 0-3 and 8-11)
  Row 5:  T20, T21, T22, T23  (cols 0-3 and 8-11)
  Row 6:  T24, T25, T26, T27  (cols 0-3 and 8-11)
  Row 7:  T28, T29, T30, T31  (cols 0-3 and 8-11)
  Row 8:  T0,  T1,  T2,  T3   (cols 0-3 and 8-11) -- same threads as row 0, different registers
  Row 9:  T4,  T5,  T6,  T7
  ...
  Row 15: T28, T29, T30, T31

REGISTER ASSIGNMENT PER THREAD (all 32 threads):
  Each thread holds exactly 4 registers (8 f16 elements total):
  - Register containing {a0,a1}: covers matrix rows 0-7,  columns 0-7
  - Register containing {a2,a3}: covers matrix rows 8-15, columns 0-7
  - Register containing {a4,a5}: covers matrix rows 0-7,  columns 8-15
  - Register containing {a6,a7}: covers matrix rows 8-15, columns 8-15

NOTE: The column coverage per thread within each row spans 4 consecutive columns (e.g., cols 0-3 for T0-T3 in row 0). The exact column offset within the 4-column span is determined by the thread's position within its group of 4. Each thread covers exactly 1 column position in each of the four 4-column sub-blocks (cols 0-3, cols 4-7 implied by pattern, cols 8-11, cols 12-15 implied by pattern).

THREAD GROUPING PATTERN SUMMARY

Each thread Tn holds exactly 8 fragments: a0 through a7.
Thread group assignment per row: row r uses threads T(4r) through T(4r+3) for r=0..7 in both the upper (rows 0-7) and lower (rows 8-15) bands.

Thread Tn (where n = 4r + c, r=0..7, c=0..3):
- a0: matrix row r,       col (n mod 4),    cols 0-3 band
- a1: matrix row r+8,     col (n mod 4),    cols 0-3 band
- a2: matrix row r,       col (n mod 4)+4,  cols 4-7 band
- a3: matrix row r+8,     col (n mod 4)+4,  cols 4-7 band
- a4: matrix row r,       col (n mod 4)+8,  cols 8-11 band

REGISTER PACKING
Since the data type is .f64 (64-bit), each fragment (a0 through a7) occupies one 64-bit register (or a pair of 32-bit registers in PTX). There is no packing of multiple values into a single register (unlike .f16 which packs two values per 32-bit register). Each fragment corresponds to exactly one double-precision scalar value.

MATRIX OPERATION AND TYPE
- Instruction: MMA .m16n8k16
- Matrix: B (input matrix)
- Data types: .f16 or .bf16
- Matrix dimensions: M=16, N=8, K=16

GRID STRUCTURE
- Rows represent matrix rows (0-15, split into two groups: rows 0-7 and rows 8-15)
- Columns represent lane/thread groupings (columns 0, 1, 2, .., 7), corresponding to N dimension (8 columns)
- The label at the bottom is: %laneid:{fragments}

THREAD-TO-ELEMENT MAPPING

The matrix B has K=16 rows and N=8 columns. The layout is divided into two horizontal bands:

UPPER BAND (Matrix rows 0-7):
- Column 0: Threads T0 through T3 (T0 at rows 0-1, T3 at rows 6-7)
  - T0 holds {b0, b1} (two elements packed in one register)
  - T1 (implied, rows 2-3): holds {b0, b1}
  - T2 (implied, rows 4-5): holds {b0, b1}
  - T3 holds {b0, b1} (rows 6-7)
- Column 1: Threads T4 through T7
  - T4 holds {b0, b1}
  - T5 (implied): holds {b0, b1}
  - T6 (implied): holds {b0, b1}
  - T7 holds {b0, b1}
- Columns 2 through 6 (implied, shown as ".."):  follow same pattern
- Column 7: Threads T28 through T31
  - T28 holds {b0, b1}
  - T29 (implied): holds {b0, b1}
  - T30 (implied): holds {b0, b1}
  - T31 holds {b0, b1}

LOWER BAND (Matrix rows 8-15):
- Column 0: Threads T0 through T3
  - T0 holds {b2, b3} (rows 8-9)
  - T1 (implied, rows 10-11): holds {b2, b3}
  - T2 (implied, rows 12-13): holds {b2, b3}
  - T3 holds {b2, b3} (rows 14-15)
- Column 1: Threads T4 through T7
  - T4 holds {b2, b3}
  - T5 (implied): holds {b2, b3}
  - T6 (implied): holds {b2, b3}
  - T7 holds {b2, b3}
- Columns 2 through 6 (implied): follow same pattern
- Column 7: Threads T28 through T31
  - T28 holds {b2, b3}
  - T29 (implied): holds {b2, b3}
  - T30 (implied): holds {b2, b3}
  - T31 holds {b2, b3}

REGISTER ELEMENT NAMING AND PACKING
- b0, b1, b2, b3 are the fragment register names for matrix B
- Each thread holds 4 elements total: b0, b1 (from upper band, rows 0-7) and b2, b3 (from lower band, rows 8-15)
- Elements are packed two per 32-bit register: {b0, b1} packed into one 32-bit register, {b2, b3} packed into one 32-bit register
- Each thread thus uses 2 registers total for matrix B
- For .f16 or .bf16: two 16-bit values are packed into each 32-bit register

GROUPING PATTERN SUMMARY
- 32 threads total (T0-T31) cover all 8 columns of matrix B
- Each group of 4 consecutive threads (T0-T3, T4-T7, ..., T28-T31) covers one column of the N=8 dimension
- Within each group of 4 threads, each thread covers 2 consecutive rows in each band (2 rows in rows 0-7, 2 rows in rows 8-15)
- Each thread owns exactly 2 elements per band (b0,b1 or b2,b3), for 4 elements total across both bands
- Column index maps to thread group: column c corresponds to threads T(4c) through T(4c+3)

MMA .m16n8k16 Fragment Layout for Matrix B with .f64 Type

1. MATRIX DIMENSIONS AND IDENTITY
Operation: MMA m16n8k16
Matrix: B (input matrix)
Data type: .f64 (64-bit double precision floating point)
Matrix B logical dimensions: 16 rows x 8 columns (K=16 rows, N=8 columns)

2. GRID STRUCTURE
The table has:
Rows (labeled 0-15): represent the 16 rows of matrix B (K dimension)
Columns (labeled 0, 1, 2, ..., 7): represent the 8 columns of matrix B (N dimension)
Entries: ThreadID:RegisterFragment notation (e.g., T0:B0 means Thread 0, register fragment B0)

3. COMPLETE THREAD-TO-ELEMENT MAPPING

The table shows columns 0, 1, and 7 explicitly. Columns 2 through 6 are implied by the downward arrows (continuation pattern).

ROWS 0-3 (first group, register B0):
Row 0: Col 0 = T0:B0,  Col 1 = T4:B0,  Col 7 = T28:B0
Row 1: Col 0 = T1:B0,  Col 1 = T5:B0,  Col 7 = T29:B0
Row 2: Col 0 = T2:B0,  Col 1 = T6:B0,  Col 7 = T30:B0
Row 3: Col 0 = T3:B0,  Col 1 = T7:B0,  Col 7 = T31:B0

ROWS 4-7 (second group, register B1):
Row 4: Col 0 = T0:B1,  Col 1 = T4:B1,  Col 7 = T28:B1
Row 5: Col 0 = T1:B1,  Col 1 = T5:B1,  Col 7 = T29:B1
Row 6: Col 0 = T2:B1,  Col 1 = T6:B1,  Col 7 = T30:B1
Row 7: Col 0 = T3:B1,  Col 1 = T7:B1,  Col 7 = T31:B1

ROWS 8-11 (third group, register B2):
Row 8:  Col 0 = T0:B2,  Col 1 = T4:B2,  Col 7 = T28:B2
Row 9:  Col 0 = T1:B2,  Col 1 = T5:B2,  Col 7 = T29:B2
Row 10: Col 0 = T2:B2,  Col 1 = T6:B2,  Col 7 = T30:B2
Row 11: Col 0 = T3:B2,  Col 1 = T7:B2,  Col 7 = T31:B2

ROWS 12-15 (fourth group, register B3):
Row 12: Col 0 = T0:B3,  Col 1 = T4:B3,  Col 7 = T28:B3
Row 13: Col 0 = T1:B3,  Col 1 = T5:B3,  Col 7 = T29:B3
Row 14: Col 0 = T2:B3,  Col 1 = T6:B3,  Col 7 = T30:B3
Row 15: Col 0 = T3:B3,  Col 1 = T7:B3,  Col 7 = T31:B3

4. DATA TYPE
.f64 = 64-bit double precision floating point
Each register fragment (B0, B1, B2, B3) holds one f64 value (one 64-bit element), stored as a pair of 32-bit registers in PTX (since PTX registers are 32-bit, each f64 occupies two consecutive 32-bit registers).

5. GROUPING PATTERNS

Column grouping (N dimension, 8 columns total):
Columns 0-7 are distributed across threads T0-T31 in groups of 4 threads per column-pair.
Each group of 4 consecutive threads (T0-T3, T4-T7, T8-T11, ..., T28-T31) covers one column of matrix B.
Specifically:
  Column 0: threads T0, T1, T2, T3 (rows 0,1,2,3 respectively within each row-group)
  Column 1: threads T4, T5, T6, T7
  Column 2: threads T8, T9, T10, T11
  Column 3: threads T12, T13, T14, T15
  Column 4: threads T16, T17, T18, T19
  Column 5: threads T20, T21, T22, T23
  Column 6: threads T24, T25, T26, T27
  Column 7: threads T28, T29, T30, T31

Row grouping (K dimension, 16 rows total):
Rows 0-3   map to register B0 for all threads
Rows 4-7   map to register B1 for all threads
Rows 8-11  map to register B2 for all threads
Rows 12-15 map to register B3 for all threads

Each thread holds 4 register fragments: B0, B1, B2, B3.

6. REGISTER PACKING
Data type is .f64 (64-bit). In PTX, f64 values require 64 bits of storage.
Each fragment Bn corresponds to one f64 element, stored in a 64-bit register (or equivalently two 32-bit registers as a register pair in PTX notation).
There is NO packing of multiple values into a single register (unlike f16 where two values share one 32-bit register). Each f64 element occupies its own 64-bit register fragment.
Each thread holds exactly 4 f64 register fragments: B0, B1, B2, B3, covering one column of matrix B across all 16 K-rows.

SUMMARY PER THREAD:
Every thread Tx (where x = 0 to 31) holds:
  B0: element at matrix B[row_group_0][col_x] where col_x = floor(x/4), row within group = x mod 4, rows 0-3
  B1: element at matrix B[row_group_1][col_x], rows 4-7
  B2: element at matrix B[row_group_2][col_x], rows 8-11
  B3: element at matrix B[row_group_3][col_x], rows 12-15

The label at the bottom "%laneid:{fragments}" confirms that the lane ID (thread index within warp, 0-31) determines which column and which row-within-group each thread owns.

Matrix B logical dimensions: 16 rows x 8 columns (K=16 rows, N=8 columns)

2. GRID STRUCTURE
The table has:
Rows (labeled 0-15): represent the 16 rows of matrix B (K dimension)
Columns (labeled 0, 1, 2, ..., 7): represent the 8 columns of matrix B (N dimension)
Entries: ThreadID:RegisterFragment notation (e.g., T0:B0 means Thread 0, register fragment B0)

3. COMPLETE THREAD-TO-ELEMENT MAPPING

The table shows columns 0, 1, and 7 explicitly. Columns 2 through 6 are implied by the downward arrows (continuation pattern).

ROWS 0-3 (first group, register B0):
Row 0: Col 0 = T0:B0,  Col 1 = T4:B0,  Col 7 = T28:B0
Row 1: Col 0 = T1:B0,  Col 1 = T5:B0,  Col 7 = T29:B0
Row 2: Col 0 = T2:B0,  Col 1 = T6:B0,  Col 7 = T30:B0
Row 3: Col 0 = T3:B0,  Col 1 = T7:B0,  Col 7 = T31:B0

ROWS 4-7 (second group, register B1):
Row 4: Col 0 = T0:B1,  Col 1 = T4:B1,  Col 7 = T28:B1
Row 5: Col 0 = T1:B1,  Col 1 = T5:B1,  Col 7 = T29:B1
Row 6: Col 0 = T2:B1,  Col 1 = T6:B1,  Col 7 = T30:B1
Row 7: Col 0 = T3:B1,  Col 1 = T7:B1,  Col 7 = T31:B1

ROWS 8-11 (third group, register B2):
Row 8:  Col 0 = T0:B2,  Col 1 = T4:B2,  Col 7 = T28:B2
Row 9:  Col 0 = T1:B2,  Col 1 = T5:B2,  Col 7 = T29:B2
Row 10: Col 0 = T2:B2,  Col 1 = T6:B2,  Col 7 = T30:B2
Row 11: Col 0 = T3:B2,  Col 1 = T7:B2,  Col 7 = T31:B2

ROWS 12-15 (fourth group, register B3):
Row 12: Col 0 = T0:B3,  Col 1 = T4:B3,  Col 7 = T28:B3
Row 13: Col 0 = T1:B3,  Col 1 = T5:B3,  Col 7 = T29:B3
Row 14: Col 0 = T2:B3,  Col 1 = T6:B3,  Col 7 = T30:B3
Row 15: Col 0 = T3:B3,  Col 1 = T7:B3,  Col 7 = T31:B3

4. DATA TYPE
.f64 = 64-bit double precision floating point
Each register fragment (B0, B1, B2, B3) holds one f64 value (one 64-bit element), stored as a pair of 32-bit registers in PTX (since PTX registers are 32-bit, each f64 occupies two consecutive 32-bit registers).

5. GROUPING PATTERNS

Column grouping (N dimension, 8 columns total):
Columns 0-7 are distributed across threads T0-T31 in groups of 4 threads per column-pair.
Each group of 4 consecutive threads (T0-T3, T4-T7, T8-T11, ..., T28-T31) covers one column of matrix B.
Specifically:
  Column 0: threads T0, T1, T2, T3 (rows 0,1,2,3 respectively within each row-group)
  Column 1: threads T4, T5, T6, T7
  Column 2: threads T8, T9, T10, T11
  Column 3: threads T12, T13, T14, T15
  Column 4: threads T16, T17, T18, T19
  Column 5: threads T20, T21, T22, T23
  Column 6: threads T24, T25, T26, T27
  Column 7: threads T28, T29, T30, T31

Row grouping (K dimension, 16 rows total):
Rows 0-3   map to register B0 for all threads
Rows 4-7   map to register B1 for all threads
Rows 8-11  map to register B2 for all threads
Rows 12-15 map to register B3 for all threads

Each thread holds 4 register fragments: B0, B1, B2, B3.

6. REGISTER PACKING
Data type is .f64 (64-bit). In PTX, f64 values require 64 bits of storage.
Each fragment Bn corresponds to one f64 element, stored in a 64-bit register (or equivalently two 32-bit registers as a register pair in PTX notation).
There is NO packing of multiple values into a single register (unlike f16 where two values share one 32-bit register). Each f64 element occupies its own 64-bit register fragment.
Each thread holds exactly 4 f64 register fragments: B0, B1, B2, B3, covering one column of matrix B across all 16 K-rows.

SUMMARY PER THREAD:
Every thread Tx (where x = 0 to 31) holds:
  B0: element at matrix B[row_group_0][col_x] where col_x = floor(x/4), row within group = x mod 4, rows 0-3
  B1: element at matrix B[row_group_1][col_x], rows 4-7
  B2: element at matrix B[row_group_2][col_x], rows 8-11
  B3: element at matrix B[row_group_3][col_x], rows 12-15

The label at the bottom "%laneid:{fragments}" confirms that the lane ID (thread index within warp, 0-31) determines which column and which row-within-group each thread owns.

    >
    >
    > Figure 82 MMA .m16n8k16 fragment layout for matrix B with `.f64` type.
    >
    > The row and column of a matrix fragment can be computed as:
    >
    > ```
    > groupID           = %laneid >> 2
    > threadID_in_group = %laneid % 4
    >
    > row =  threadID_in_group + (i * 4)           for bi where  i < 4
    >
    > col =  groupID
    > ```
* Accumulators (C or D):

  | .ctype / .dtype | Fragment | Elements (low to high) |
  | --- | --- | --- |
  | `.f64` | A vector expression containing four `.f64` registers containing `.f64` elements from the matrix C (or D). | c0, c1, c2, c3 |
  | `.f32` | A vector expression containing four `.f32` registers containing four `.f32` elements from the matrix C (or D). |
  | `.f16` | A vector expression containing two `.f16x2` registers, with each register containing two `.f16` elements from the matrix C (or D). |

  The layout of the fragments held by different threads is shown in [Figure 83](#mma-16816-c).

   UPPER HALF OF MATRIX (rows 0-7), registers c0 and c1:

   Row 15, cols 0-1: T28 holds {c2, c3}

##### 9.7.14.5.9. [Matrix Fragments for `mma.m16n8k16` with integer type](#warp-level-matrix-fragment-mma-16816-i8-f8)

A warp executing `mma.m16n8k16` will compute an MMA operation of shape `.m16n8k16`.

Elements of the matrix are distributed across the threads in a warp so each thread of the warp holds
a fragment of the matrix.

* Multiplicand A:

  | .atype | Fragment | Elements (low to high) |
  | --- | --- | --- |
  | `.u8` / `.s8` | A vector expression containing two `.b32` registers, with each register containing four `.u8` / `.s8` elements from the matrix A. | a0, a1, a2, a3, a4, a5, a6, a7 |
  | `.e4m3` / `.e5m2` | A vector expression containing two `.b32` registers, with each register containing four `.e4m3` / `.e5m2` elements from the matrix A. | a0, a1, a2, a3, a4, a5, a6, a7 |

  The layout of the fragments held by different threads is shown in [Figure 84](#mma-16816-a-i8).

  Figure 85 MMA .m16n8k16 fragment layout for matrix B with `.u8` / `.s8` type.

  The row and column of a matrix fragment can be computed as:

  ```
  groupID           = %laneid >> 2
  threadID_in_group = %laneid % 4

  row =  (threadID_in_group * 4) + i         for bi where i = {0,..,3}

  col = groupID
  ```
* Accumulators (C or D):

  | .ctype / .dtype | Fragment | Elements (low to high) |
  | --- | --- | --- |
  | `.s32` | A vector expression containing four `.s32` registers, containing four `.s32` elements from the matrix C (or D). | c0, c1, c2, c3 |
  | `.f32` | A vector expression containing four `.f32` registers, containing four `.f32` elements from the matrix C (or D). | c0, c1, c2, c3 |
  | `.f16` | A vector expression containing two `.f16x2` registers, with each register containing two `.f16` elements from the matrix C (or D). | c0, c1, c1, c2 |

  The layout of the fragments held by different threads is shown in [Figure 86](#mma-16816-c-i8).

  ```

##### 9.7.14.5.10. [Matrix Fragments for `mma.m16n8k32`](#warp-level-matrix-fragment-mma-16832)

A warp executing `mma.m16n8k32` will compute an MMA operation of shape `.m16n8k32`.

Elements of the matrix are distributed across the threads in a warp so each thread of the warp holds
a fragment of the matrix.

* Multiplicand A:

  + `.s4` or `.u4` :

    > | .atype | Fragment | Elements (low to high) |
    > | --- | --- | --- |
    > | `.s4` / `.u4` | A vector expression containing two `.b32` registers, with each register containing eight `.u4` / `.s4` elements from the matrix A. | a0, a1, …, a14, a15 |
    >
    > The layout of the fragments held by different threads is shown in [Figure 87](#mma-16832-a-i4).
    >
    >

- All 32 threads (T0-T31) are used, each assigned to exactly one row in each half (rows 0-7 and rows 8-15).

OVERVIEW
Matrix: B
Operation: MMA m16n8k32
Data type: .u4 (unsigned 4-bit integer) or .s4 (signed 4-bit integer)
Matrix B dimensions: K x N = 32 rows x 8 columns

GRID STRUCTURE
The table has:
- Rows (vertical axis): represent matrix row ranges (0-7, 8-15, 16-23, 24-31), corresponding to K dimension
- Columns (horizontal axis): represent matrix column indices 0, 1, 2, .., 7 (N dimension, 8 columns total)
- Columns 0 and 1 are explicitly shown; column 2 and columns up to 7 are indicated with ".." notation
- Column 7 is explicitly shown

THREAD ASSIGNMENT BY ROW BAND AND COLUMN

Row band 0-7 (K rows 0 through 7):
  Column 0: Thread T0, holds elements b0, b1, .., b6, b7
  Column 1: Thread T4, holds elements b0, b1, .., b6, b7
  Column 2: (implied, follows pattern)
  ...
  Column 7: Thread T28, holds elements b0, b1, .., b6, b7

Row band 8-15 (K rows 8 through 15):
  Column 0: Thread T1, holds elements b0, b1, .., b6, b7
  Column 1: Thread T5, holds elements b0, b1, .., b6, b7
  Column 2: (implied, follows pattern)
  ...
  Column 7: Thread T29, holds elements b0, b1, .., b6, b7

Row band 16-23 (K rows 16 through 23):
  Column 0: Thread T2, holds elements b0, b1, .., b6, b7
  Column 1: Thread T6, holds elements b0, b1, .., b6, b7
  Column 2: (implied, follows pattern)
  ...
  Column 7: Thread T30, holds elements b0, b1, .., b6, b7

Row band 24-31 (K rows 24 through 31):
  Column 0: Thread T3, holds elements b0, b1, .., b6, b7
  Column 1: Thread T7, holds elements b0, b1, .., b6, b7
  Column 2: (implied, follows pattern)
  ...
  Column 7: Thread T31, holds elements b0, b1, .., b6, b7

THREAD NUMBERING PATTERN
- Threads T0-T3 cover columns 0, rows 0-7 / 8-15 / 16-23 / 24-31 respectively
- Threads T4-T7 cover column 1, same row band pattern
- Each subsequent group of 4 threads covers the next column
- Thread Tx for column c, row band r: T = c*4 + r (where r = 0,1,2,3 for row bands 0-7, 8-15, 16-23, 24-31)
- Total threads used: T0 through T31 (32 threads)

REGISTER ELEMENT PACKING
Each thread holds 8 elements labeled b0 through b7 within a single register (shown as a column vector).
Since the data type is 4-bit (.u4 or .s4), 8 elements of 4 bits each = 32 bits total, fitting exactly into one 32-bit register.
So each thread holds exactly 1 register containing 8 packed 4-bit values (b0, b1, b2, b3, b4, b5, b6, b7).

ELEMENT COVERAGE PER THREAD
Each thread covers 8 consecutive K-rows within its assigned row band and one specific N-column.
The 8 elements b0-b7 correspond to the 8 matrix rows within that thread's row band for its assigned column.

ARROW NOTATION
An arrow is drawn from the cell at row band 0-7 / column 2 area down to row band 24-31 / column 2 area, indicating the ".." columns follow the same pattern as the explicitly shown columns.

Row Band  | Col 0 | Col 1 | Col 2 | ... | Col 7
0-7       |  T0   |  T4   |  T8   | ... |  T28
8-15      |  T1   |  T5   |  T9   | ... |  T29
16-23     |  T2   |  T6   |  T10  | ... |  T30
24-31     |  T3   |  T7   |  T11  | ... |  T31

Each thread holds 1 register with 8 packed 4-bit values (b0-b7), totaling 32 bits per register.

THREAD ASSIGNMENT PATTERN:
- Threads T0-T3 cover matrix rows 0-3, 4-7, 8-11, 12-15 respectively at column group 0
- Threads T4-T7 cover the same row groups at column group 1
- Threads T8-T11 cover the same row groups at column group 2 (implied)
- Threads T28-T31 cover the same row groups at column group 7

General formula:
- Thread index = (col_group * 4) + row_group
  where row_group = 0 for rows 0-3, 1 for rows 4-7, 2 for rows 8-11, 3 for rows 12-15
  and col_group = 0..7

REGISTER PACKING:
Each thread holds 4 elements: b0, b1, b2, b3
These 4 elements are packed into registers as a group (shown as a single brace notation {b0, b1, b2, b3}).
For 8-bit types (u8/s8) or sub-byte types (e4m3/e5m2/e3m2/e2m3/e2m1), multiple elements are packed into each 32-bit register. Specifically, 4 elements of 8-bit type fit into one 32-bit register (b0, b1, b2, b3 packed into one register). For sub-byte types, even more elements fit per register.

SUMMARY:
- 32 threads total (T0-T31) cover the full B fragment
- Each thread holds one register containing 4 packed elements (b0-b3)
- Column dimension (N=8, cols 0-7) is distributed across groups of 4 threads (T0,T4,T8,...,T28 for row group 0; T1,T5,T9,...,T29 for row group 1; etc.)
- Row dimension (rows 0-15, grouped into 4 groups of 4 rows) is distributed across threads within each group of 4 (T0-T3 at col 0, T4-T7 at col 1, etc.)
- The arrow in the figure indicates the direction of the %laneid:{fragments} axis spanning columns 0 through 7

  The row and column of a matrix fragment can be computed as:

  ```
  groupID           = %laneid >> 2
  threadID_in_group = %laneid % 4

  row =      groupID                           for ci where i <  2
           groupID + 8                         for ci where i >= 2

  col =  (threadID_in_group * 2) + (i & 0x1)    for ci where i = {0,..,3}
  ```

##### 9.7.14.5.11. [Matrix Fragments for `mma.m16n8k64`](#warp-level-matrix-fragment-mma-16864)

A warp executing `mma.m16n8k64` will compute an MMA operation of shape `.m16n8k64`.

Elements of the matrix are distributed across the threads in a warp so each thread of the warp holds
a fragment of the matrix.

* Multiplicand A:

  | .atype | Fragment | Elements (low to high) |
  | --- | --- | --- |
  | `.s4` / `.u4` | A vector expression containing four `.b32` registers, with each register containing eight `.s4` / `.u4` elements from the matrix A. | a0, a1, …, a30, a31 |

  Figure 93 MMA .m16n8k64 fragment layout for matrix A with `.u4` / `.s4` / `.e2m1` type.

  The row and column of a matrix fragment can be computed as:

  ```
  groupID           = %laneid >> 2
  threadID_in_group = %laneid % 4

  row =     groupID                                     for ai where 0 <= i < 8 || 16 <= i < 24
          groupID + 8                                   otherwise

  col =      (threadID_in_group * 8) + (i & 0x7)        for ai where i < 16
             (threadID_in_group * 8) + (i & 0x7) + 32   for ai where i >= 16
  ```

  | .btype | Fragment | Elements (low to high) |
  | --- | --- | --- |
  | `.s4` / `.u4` | A vector expression containing two `.b32` registers, with each register containing eight `.s4` / `.u4` elements from the matrix B. | b0, b1, …, b14, b15 |
  | `.e2m1` | A vector expression containing two `.b32` registers, with each register containing eight `.e2m1` elements from the matrix B. | b0, b1, …, b14, b15 |

This figure shows the SECOND half of matrix B (rows 32-63); the first half covers rows 0-31.

GRID STRUCTURE:
- Rows (vertical axis): Matrix rows 32-63, divided into 4 bands of 8 rows each
- Columns (horizontal axis): Columns 0, 1, 2, .., 7 (8 columns total, indices 0 through 7)
- Threads shown: T0 through T31 (32 threads total)

TABLE OF THREAD-TO-ELEMENT MAPPING:

Row \ Col |  0   |  1   |  2  | .. |  7
----------|------|------|-----|----|------
32-39     | T0   | T4   | ... | .. | T28
40-47     | T1   | T5   | ... | .. | T29
48-55     | T2   | T6   | ... | .. | T30
56-63(64) | T3   | T7   | ... | .. | T31

THREAD GROUPING PATTERN:
- Threads T0, T1, T2, T3 cover columns 0 for row bands 32-39, 40-47, 48-55, 56-63 respectively
- Threads T4, T5, T6, T7 cover column 1 for the same row bands respectively
- This pattern continues: threads T(4*col + row_band) cover (col, row_band)
- Threads T28, T29, T30, T31 cover column 7 for row bands 32-39, 40-47, 48-55, 56-63 respectively

REGISTER ELEMENTS PER THREAD:
Each thread holds a register group containing elements: b8, b9, .., b14, b15
Specifically, each thread's register contains 6 named elements (shown as a curly-brace vector):
  - b8
  - b9
  - .. (intermediate elements)
  - b14
  - b15

So each thread holds elements b8 through b15 (8 elements total: b8, b9, b10, b11, b12, b13, b14, b15).

ELEMENT PACKING:
- Data types are 4-bit (.u4/.s4) or 2-bit-mantissa (.e2m1)
- Multiple elements are packed into 32-bit registers
- For 4-bit types: 8 elements pack into one 32-bit register (b8 through b15 fit in one register)
- Elements b8-b15 represent the second set of 8 packed values (registers b0-b7 would be in rows 0-31)
- The register indices b8-b15 indicate these are the second register group (registers 8 through 15 of the B fragment)

DIAGONAL ARROW NOTE:
A diagonal arrow is drawn from the cell at approximately (col=2, rows=32-39) pointing down to (col=2, rows=56-63), indicating the continuation/ordering pattern of thread assignment across row bands within a column group.

T0:  rows 32-39, col 0, elements b8-b15
T1:  rows 40-47, col 0, elements b8-b15
T2:  rows 48-55, col 0, elements b8-b15
T3:  rows 56-63, col 0, elements b8-b15
T4:  rows 32-39, col 1, elements b8-b15
T5:  rows 40-47, col 1, elements b8-b15
T6:  rows 48-55, col 1, elements b8-b15
T7:  rows 56-63, col 1, elements b8-b15
... (pattern continues for cols 2-6) ...
T28: rows 32-39, col 7, elements b8-b15
T29: rows 40-47, col 7, elements b8-b15
T30: rows 48-55, col 7, elements b8-b15
T31: rows 56-63, col 7, elements b8-b15


  Figure 95 MMA .m16n8k64 fragment layout for rows 32–63 of matrix B with `.u4` / `.s4` / `.e2m1` type.

  The row and column of a matrix fragment can be computed as:

  ```
  groupID           = %laneid >> 2
  threadID_in_group = %laneid % 4

  row =      (threadID_in_group * 8) + (i & 0x7)          for bi where i < 8
             (threadID_in_group * 8) + (i & 0x7) + 32     for bi where i >= 8

  col =   groupID
  ```
* Accumulators (C or D):

  | .ctype / .dtype | Fragment | Elements (low to high) |
  | --- | --- | --- |
  | `.s32` | A vector expression containing four `.s32` registers, containing four `.s32` elements from the matrix C (or D). | c0, c1, c2, c3 |
  | `.f32` | A vector expression containing four `.f32` registers, containing four `.f32` elements from the matrix C (or D). | c0, c1, c2, c3 |

##### 9.7.14.5.12. [Matrix Fragments for `mma.m16n8k128`](#warp-level-matrix-fragment-mma-168128)

A warp executing `mma.m16n8k128` will compute an MMA operation of shape `.m16n8k128`.

Elements of the matrix are distributed across the threads in a warp so each thread of the warp holds
a fragment of the matrix.

* Multiplicand A:

  | .atype | Fragment | Elements (low to high) |
  | --- | --- | --- |
  | `.b1` | A vector expression containing two `.b32` registers, with each register containing thirty two `.b1` elements from the matrix A. | a0, a1, …, a62, a63 |

  The layout of the fragments held by different threads is shown in [Figure 97](#mma-168128-a).

4. DATA TYPE
   .b1 (1-bit binary values). Since K=128 and each thread holds 32 elements (a0..a31 or a32..a63), and 32 bits fit in one 32-bit register, each register holds 32 packed 1-bit values. Thus each thread holds 2 registers total (one for a0..a31, one for a32..a63).

5. GROUPING PATTERNS
   - Threads T0..T31 are each assigned to exactly one (row, column-group) cell.
   - Rows 0..7 use matrix rows 0..7; threads T0..T31 are distributed 4 threads per row (one per column group), cycling T0-T3 for row 0, T4-T7 for row 1, ..., T28-T31 for row 7.
   - Rows 8..15 reuse the same threads T0..T31 in the same pattern, but with the second register set (a32..a63).
   - Each thread covers all 32 columns within its assigned column group (32 columns wide per group).

6. PACKING WITHIN REGISTERS
   - Data type is .b1 (1-bit).
   - 32 consecutive 1-bit elements are packed into a single 32-bit register.
   - Each thread holds 2 registers: one register containing elements a0..a31 (32 bits = 32 x 1-bit values), and one register containing elements a32..a63 (32 bits = 32 x 1-bit values).
   - Total per thread: 64 bits (2 x 32-bit registers) covering 64 matrix elements across 128 columns (32 columns per column-group x 4 column-groups, but each thread is responsible for only one column-group of 32 columns, contributing 32 elements per register set).

   Each cell contains a thread identifier and its register fragment

   The 32 threads (T0 through T31) are arranged as follows:

   Row group 0-31 (K rows 0 through 31):
     Col 0: T0  holds b0, b1, .., b31
     Col 1: T4  holds b0, b1, .., b31
     Col 2: (implied continuation)
     ..
     Col 7: T28 holds b0, b1, .., b31

   Row group 32-63 (K rows 32 through 63):
     Col 0: T1  holds b0, b1, .., b31
     Col 1: T5  holds b0, b1, .., b31
     Col 2: (implied continuation)
     ..
     Col 7: T29 holds b0, b1, .., b31

   Row group 64-95 (K rows 64 through 95):
     Col 0: T2  holds b0, b1, .., b31
     Col 1: T6  holds b0, b1, .., b31
     Col 2: (implied continuation)
     ..
     Col 7: T30 holds b0, b1, .., b31

   Row group 96-127 (K rows 96 through 127):
     Col 0: T3  holds b0, b1, .., b31
     Col 1: T7  holds b0, b1, .., b31
     Col 2: (implied continuation)
     ..

   Each thread holds elements named b0 through b31 (32 elements per thread).
   These are labeled b0, b1, .., b31 within each thread's fragment vector.

   Threads are grouped by column (N dimension) in steps of 4:
     Column 0: T0, T1, T2, T3  (covering all 4 row groups = K rows 0-127)

     Column 2: T8, T9, T10, T11
     Column 3: T12, T13, T14, T15
     Column 4: T16, T17, T18, T19
     Column 5: T20, T21, T22, T23
     Column 6: T24, T25, T26, T27
     Column 7: T28, T29, T30, T31

     Thread offset 1 (e.g., T1): K rows 32-63
     Thread offset 2 (e.g., T2): K rows 64-95
     Thread offset 3 (e.g., T3): K rows 96-127

   Each thread holds 32 elements (b0 through b31).

   The table has:
   Rows (Row\Col header): groups of matrix rows labeled 0-31, 32-63, 64-95, 96-127
   Columns: 0, 1, 2, .., 7 (8 columns total, representing N=8 dimension)
   Each cell contains a thread identifier and its register fragment

3. THREAD-TO-ELEMENT MAPPING

   The 32 threads (T0 through T31) are arranged as follows:

   Row group 0-31 (K rows 0 through 31):
     Col 0: T0  holds b0, b1, .., b31
     Col 1: T4  holds b0, b1, .., b31
     Col 2: (implied continuation)
     ..
     Col 7: T28 holds b0, b1, .., b31

   Row group 32-63 (K rows 32 through 63):
     Col 0: T1  holds b0, b1, .., b31
     Col 1: T5  holds b0, b1, .., b31
     Col 2: (implied continuation)
     ..
     Col 7: T29 holds b0, b1, .., b31

   Row group 64-95 (K rows 64 through 95):
     Col 0: T2  holds b0, b1, .., b31
     Col 1: T6  holds b0, b1, .., b31
     Col 2: (implied continuation)
     ..
     Col 7: T30 holds b0, b1, .., b31

   Row group 96-127 (K rows 96 through 127):
     Col 0: T3  holds b0, b1, .., b31
     Col 1: T7  holds b0, b1, .., b31
     Col 2: (implied continuation)
     ..
     Col 7: T31 holds b0, b1, .., b31

4. REGISTER ELEMENT NAMING
   Each thread holds elements named b0 through b31 (32 elements per thread).
   These are labeled b0, b1, .., b31 within each thread's fragment vector.

5. GROUPING PATTERNS
   Threads are grouped by column (N dimension) in steps of 4:
     Column 0: T0, T1, T2, T3  (covering all 4 row groups = K rows 0-127)
     Column 1: T4, T5, T6, T7
     Column 2: T8, T9, T10, T11
     Column 3: T12, T13, T14, T15
     Column 4: T16, T17, T18, T19
     Column 5: T20, T21, T22, T23
     Column 6: T24, T25, T26, T27
     Column 7: T28, T29, T30, T31

   Within each column group, the 4 threads cover consecutive 32-row bands of K:
     Thread offset 0 (e.g., T0): K rows 0-31
     Thread offset 1 (e.g., T1): K rows 32-63
     Thread offset 2 (e.g., T2): K rows 64-95
     Thread offset 3 (e.g., T3): K rows 96-127

6. PACKING WITHIN REGISTERS
   Each thread holds 32 elements (b0 through b31).
   Since the data type is .b1 (1-bit), 32 bits pack into a single 32-bit register.
   Therefore each thread holds exactly 1 register (32-bit) containing 32 packed 1-bit values.
   Total bits per thread: 32 x 1-bit = 32 bits = 1 x 32-bit register.

7. ANNOTATION
   The figure includes a label at the bottom: %laneid:{fragment}
   indicating that the lane ID (thread index within the warp) determines which fragment is held.
   An arrow in column 2 points downward from row group 0-31 to row group 96-127, illustrating the direction of increasing row index within the K dimension.


  Figure 98 MMA .m16n8k128 fragment layout for matrix B with `.b1` type.

  The row and column of a matrix fragment can be computed as:

  ```
  groupID           = %laneid >> 2
  threadID_in_group = %laneid % 4

  row =  (threadID_in_group * 32) + i         for bi where i = {0,...,31}
  col = groupID
  ```
* Accumulators (C or D):

  | .ctype / .dtype | Fragment | Elements (low to high) |
  | --- | --- | --- |
  | `.s32` | A vector expression containing four `.s32` registers, containing four `.s32` elements from the matrix C (or D). | c0, c1, c2, c3 |

  The layout of the fragments held by different threads is shown in [Figure 99](#mma-168128-c).

  The row and column of a matrix fragment can be computed as:

  ```
  groupID           = %laneid >> 2
  threadID_in_group = %laneid % 4

  row =      groupID                           for ci where i <  2

  col =  (threadID_in_group * 2) + (i & 0x1)    for ci where i = {0, 1, 2, 3}
  ```

##### 9.7.14.5.13. [Matrix Fragments for `mma.m16n8k256`](#warp-level-matrix-fragment-mma-168256)

A warp executing `mma.m16n8k256` will compute an MMA operation of shape `.m16n8k256`.

Elements of the matrix are distributed across the threads in a warp so each thread of the warp holds
a fragment of the matrix.

* Multiplicand A:

  | .atype | Fragment | Elements (low to high) |
  | --- | --- | --- |
  | `.b1` | A vector expression containing four `.b32` registers, with each register containing thirty two `.b1` elements from the matrix A. | a0, a1, …, a126, a127 |

  The layout of the fragments held by different threads is shown in [Figure 100](#mma-168256-a).

     C=0..31:   T4:{a32, .., a63}
     C=32..63:  T5:{a32, .., a63}
     C=64..95:  T6:{a32, .., a63}
     C=96..127: T7:{a32, .., a63}

   Rows 10..14: continuation pattern (threads T8..T27)

   Row 15:
     C=0..31:   T28:{a32, .., a63}
     C=32..63:  T29:{a32, .., a63}
     C=64..95:  T30:{a32, .., a63}
     C=96..127: T31:{a32, .., a63}

   LOWER-RIGHT QUADRANT (Rows 8..15, Columns 128..255):
   Each thread holds elements a96 through a127 (32 elements).

   Row 8:
     C=128..159: T0:{a96, .., a127}
     C=160..191: T1:{a96, .., a127}
     C=192..223: T2:{a96, .., a127}
     C=224..255: T3:{a96, .., a127}

   Row 9:
     C=128..159: T4:{a96, .., a127}
     C=160..191: T5:{a96, .., a127}
     C=192..223: T6:{a96, .., a127}
     C=224..255: T7:{a96, .., a127}

   Rows 10..14: continuation pattern (threads T8..T27)

   Row 15:
     C=128..159: T28:{a96, .., a127}
     C=160..191: T29:{a96, .., a127}
     C=192..223: T30:{a96, .., a127}
     C=224..255: T31:{a96, .., a127}

5. GROUPING PATTERNS
   Rows 0..7 (upper half): All 32 threads (T0..T31) each cover 4 consecutive 32-column bands across K=0..127 and K=128..255.
   Rows 8..15 (lower half): Same 32 threads (T0..T31) cover the second set of element indices (a32..a63 and a96..a127).
   Within each row group of 8 rows, threads T0..T3 cover row 0, T4..T7 cover row 1, ..., T28..T31 cover row 7.
   Each thread covers exactly one matrix row and one 32-column K-band per quadrant.

6. ELEMENT PACKING WITHIN REGISTERS
   Data type is .b1 (1-bit binary).
   Each thread holds 32 one-bit elements per fragment group (e.g., a0..a31, a32..a63, a64..a95, a96..a127).
   32 one-bit elements pack into a single 32-bit register (one bit per element).
   Each thread therefore holds 4 groups of 32 bits = 4 registers total for the full K=256 dimension:
     Register group 1: a0..a31 (columns 0..31 of K, rows 0..7)
     Register group 2: a32..a63 (columns 32..63 of K, rows 8..15 mapping)
     Register group 3: a64..a95 (columns 128..159 of K, rows 0..7)
     Register group 4: a96..a127 (columns 160..191 of K, rows 8..15 mapping)

7. LANE ID NOTATION
   The figure footer states: %laneid:{fragments}
   This confirms that the thread index corresponds directly to the lane ID within the warp (lanes 0..31).


  Figure 100 MMA .m16n8k256 fragment layout for matrix A with `.b1` type.

  The row and column of a matrix fragment can be computed as:

  ```
  groupID = %laneid >> 2
  threadID_in_group = %laneid % 4

  row =   groupID                                            for ai where 0 <= i < 32 || 64 <= i < 96
          groupID + 8                                        otherwise

  col =      (threadID_in_group * 32) + i                    for ai where i < 64
             (threadID_in_group * 32) + (i & 0x1F) + 128     for ai where i >= 64
  ```

  | --- | --- | --- |
  | `.b1` | A vector expression containing two `.b32` registers, with each register containing thirty two `.b1` elements from the matrix B. | b0, b1, …, b62, b63 |

##### 9.7.14.5.14. [Multiply-and-Accumulate Instruction: `mma`](#warp-level-matrix-instructions-mma)

`mma`

Perform matrix multiply-and-accumulate operation

Syntax

Half precision floating point type:

```

Description

Perform a `MxNxK` matrix multiply and accumulate operation, `D = A*B+C`, where the A matrix is
`MxK`, the B matrix is `KxN`, and the C and D matrices are `MxN`.

Qualifier `.block_scale` specifies that the matrices A and B are scaled with `scale_A` and
`scale_B` matrices respectively before performing the matrix multiply and accumulate operation
as specified in the section [Block Scaling for mma.sync](#warp-level-block-scaling). The data type
corresponding to each of the element within `scale_A` and `Scale_B` matrices is specified
by `.stype`. Qualifier `.scale_vec_size` specifies the number of columns of `scale_A` matrix
and number of rows in the matrix `scale_B`.

The valid combinations of `.kind`, `.stype` and `.scale_vec_size` are described in
[Table 36](#mma-scaling-kind-type-valid-combination). For `mma` with `.kind::mxf4` when the
qualifier `.scale_vec_size` is not specified, then it defaults to `2X`. In contrast, when
`.kind` is specified as `.kind::mxf8f6f4` then the qualifier `.scale_vec_size` defaults
to `1X`. However, for `.kind::mxf4nvf4`, it is mandatory to provide valid `.scale_vec_size`.

A warp executing `mma.sync.m8n8k4` instruction computes 4 matrix multiply and accumulate
operations. Rest of the `mma.sync` operations compute a single matrix mutliply and accumulate
operation per warp.

For single-bit `mma.sync`, multiplication is replaced by a sequence of logical operations;
specifically, `mma.xor.popc` and `mma.and.popc` computes the XOR, AND respectively of a k-bit
row of A with a k-bit column of B, then counts the number of set bits in the result (`popc`). This
result is added to the corresponding element of C and written into D.

Operands `a` and `b` represent two multiplicand matrices A and B, while `c` and `d`
represent the accumulator and destination matrices, distributed across the threads in warp.
When `.block_scale` qualifier is specified, operand `scale-a-data`, `scale-b-data` represents
the scale matrix metadata corresponding to `scale_A` and `scale_B` matrices respectively. The
tuple `{byte-id-a, thread-id-a}` and `{byte-id-b, thread-id-b}` represent selectors for matrices
`scale_A` and `scale_B` respectively from their corresponding metadata arguments `scale-a-data`,
`scale-b-data`. The operands `scale-a-data`, `scale-b-data` are of type `.b32`. The operands
`byte-id-a`, `thread-id-a`, `byte-id-b`, `thread-id-b` are unsigned 16-bit integer values.
For more details on selector arguments refer [Block Scaling for mma.sync](#warp-level-block-scaling) section.

The registers in each thread hold a fragment of matrix as described in
[Matrix multiply-accumulate operation using mma instruction](#warp-level-matrix-instructions-for-mma).

The qualifiers `.dtype`, `.atype`, `.btype` and `.ctype` indicate the data-type of the
elements in the matrices D, A, B and C respectively. The qualifier `.stype` indicate the data-type
of the elements in the matrices `scale_A` and `scale_B`. Specific shapes have type restrictions :

* `.m8n8k4` : When `.ctype` is `.f32`, `.dtype` must also be `.f32`.
* `.m16n8k8` :

  + `.dtype` must be the same as `.ctype`.
  + `.atype` must be the same as `.btype`.
* `.m16n8k16` and `.m16n8k32` :

  + `.dtype` must be the same as `.ctype`.

The qualifiers `.alayout` and `.blayout` indicate the row-major or column-major layouts of
matrices A and B respectively.

When `.kind` is either of `.kind::mxf8f6f4` or `.kind::f8f6f4`, the individual 4-bit and the
6-bit floating point type elements must be packed in an 8-bit container. The matrix element of type
`.e2m1` resides in central 4 bits of the 8-bit container with padding in the upper 2 bits and
lower 2 bits of the container. When the matrix element is of type `.e3m2` or `.e2m3`, the
matrix element resides in the lower 6 bits of the 8-bit container with padding in the upper 2 bits
of the container. In contrast, note that when using `mma` with `.kind::mxf4` or
`.kind::mxf4nvf4`, no explicit padding is necessary even though matrix elements are of type `.e2m1`.

Precision and rounding :
:   * `.f16` floating point operations:

      Element-wise multiplication of matrix A and B is performed with at least single
      precision. When `.ctype` or `.dtype` is `.f32`, accumulation of the intermediate values
      is performed with at least single precision. When both `.ctype` and `.dtype` are specified
      as `.f16`, the accumulation is performed with at least half precision.

      The accumulation order, rounding and handling of subnormal inputs are unspecified.
    * `.e4m3`, `.e5m2`, `.e3m2`, `.e2m3`, `.e2m1` floating point operations :

      Element-wise multiplication of matrix A and B is performed with specified precision. Accumulation
      of the intermediate values is performed with at least single precision.

      The accumulation order, rounding, and handling of subnormal inputs are unspecified.
    * `.bf16` and `.tf32` floating point operations :

      Element-wise multiplication of matrix A and B is performed with specified
      precision. Accumulation of the intermediate values is performed with at least single
      precision.

      The accumulation order, rounding, and handling of subnormal inputs are unspecified.
    * `.f64` floating point operations :

      Precision of the element-wise multiplication and addition operation is identical to that of `.f64`
      precision fused multiply-add. Supported rounding modifiers are :

      + `.rn` : mantissa LSB rounds to nearest even. This is the default.
      + `.rz` : mantissa LSB rounds towards zero.
      + `.rm` : mantissa LSB rounds towards negative infinity.
      + `.rp` : mantissa LSB rounds towards positive infinity.
    * Integer operations :

      The integer `mma` operation is performed with `.s32` accumulators. The `.satfinite`
      qualifier indicates that on overflow, the accumulated value is limited to the range
      *MIN_INT32*.. *MAX_INT32* (where the bounds are defined as the minimum negative signed 32-bit
      integer and the maximum positive signed 32-bit integer respectively).

      If `.satfinite` is not specified, the accumulated value is wrapped instead.

The mandatory `.sync` qualifier indicates that `mma` instruction causes the executing thread to
wait until all threads in the warp execute the same `mma` instruction before resuming execution.

The mandatory `.aligned` qualifier indicates that all threads in the warp must execute the same
`mma` instruction. In conditionally executed code, a `mma` instruction should only be used if it
is known that all threads in the warp evaluate the condition identically, otherwise behavior is
undefined.

The behavior of `mma` instruction is undefined if all threads in the same warp do not use the same
qualifiers, or if any thread in the warp has exited.

Notes

Programs using double precision floating point `mma` instruction with shapes `.m16n8k4`,
`.m16n8k8`, and `.m16n8k16` require at least 64 registers for compilation.

PTX ISA Notes

Introduced in PTX ISA version 6.4.

`.f16` floating point type `mma` operation with `.m8n8k4` shape introduced in PTX ISA version
6.4.

`.f16` floating point type `mma` operation with `.m16n8k8` shape introduced in PTX ISA version
6.5.

`.u8/.s8` integer type `mma` operation with `.m8n8k16` shape introduced in PTX ISA version
6.5.

`.u4/.s4` integer type `mma` operation with `.m8n8k32` shape introduced in PTX ISA version
6.5.

`.f64` floating point type `mma` operation with `.m8n8k4` shape introduced in PTX ISA version
7.0.

`.f16` floating point type `mma` operation with `.m16n8k16` shape introduced in PTX ISA
version 7.0.

`.bf16` alternate floating point type `mma` operation with `.m16n8k8` and `.m16n8k16` shapes
introduced in PTX ISA version 7.0.

`.tf32` alternate floating point type `mma` operation with `.m16n8k4` and `.m16n8k8` shapes
introduced in PTX ISA version 7.0.

`.u8/.s8` integer type `mma` operation with `.m16n8k16` and `.m16n8k32` shapes introduced in
PTX ISA version 7.0.

`.u4/.s4` integer type `mma` operation with `.m16n8k32` and `.m16n8k64` shapes introduced in
PTX ISA version 7.0.

`.b1` single-bit integer type `mma` operation with `.m8n8k128`, `.m16n8k128` and
`.m16n8k256` shapes introduced in PTX ISA version 7.0.

Support for `.and` operation in single-bit `mma` introduced in PTX ISA version 7.1.

`.f64` floating point type `mma` operation with `.m16n8k4`, `.m16n8k8`, and `.m16n8k16`
shapes introduced in PTX ISA version 7.8.

Support for `.e4m3` and `.e5m2` alternate floating point type `mma` operation introduced in
PTX ISA version 8.4.

Support for shape `.m16n8k16` and `.f16` `dtype`/`ctype` with `.e4m3`/`.e5m2` alternate
floating point type mma operation introduced in PTX ISA version 8.7.

Support for `.e3m2`, `.e2m3`, `.e2m1` alternate floating point type `mma` operation introduced
in PTX ISA version 8.7.

Support for `.kind`, `.block_scale`, `.scale_vec_size` qualifier introduced in PTX ISA version 8.7.

Support for `.scale_vec::4X` on `.ue8m0` as `.stype` with `.kind::mxf4nvf4` is introduced in
PTX ISA version 9.1.

Target ISA Notes

Requires `sm_70` or higher.

`.f16` floating point type `mma` operation with `.m8n8k4` shape requires `sm_70` or higher.

Note

`mma.sync.m8n8k4` is optimized for target architecture `sm_70` and may have substantially
reduced performance on other target architectures.

`.f16` floating point type `mma` operation with `.m16n8k8` shape requires `sm_75` or higher.

`.u8/.s8` integer type `mma` operation with `.m8n8k16` shape requires `sm_75` or higher.

`.u4/.s4` integer type `mma` operation with `.m8n8k32` shape `sm_75` or higher.

`.b1` single-bit integer type `mma` operation with `.m8n8k128` shape `sm_75` or higher.

`.f64` floating point type `mma` operation with `.m8n8k4` shape requires `sm_80` or higher.

`.f16` floating point type `mma` operation with `.m16n8k16` shape requires `sm_80` or
higher.

`.bf16` alternate floating point type `mma` operation with `.m16n8k8` and `.m16n8k16` shapes
requires `sm_80` or higher.

`.tf32` alternate floating point type `mma` operation with `.m16n8k4` and `.m16n8k8` shapes
requires `sm_80` or higher.

`.u8/.s8` integer type `mma` operation with `.m16n8k16` and `.m16n8k32` shapes requires
`sm_80` or higher.

`.u4/.s4` integer type `mma` operation with `.m16n8k32` and `.m16n8k64` shapes requires
`sm_80` or higher.

`.b1` single-bit integer type `mma` operation with `.m16n8k128` and `.m16n8k256` shapes
requires `sm_80` or higher.

`.and` operation in single-bit `mma` requires `sm_80` or higher.

`.f64` floating point type `mma` operation with `.m16n8k4`, `.m16n8k8`, and `.m16n8k16`
shapes require `sm_90` or higher.

`.e4m3` and `.e5m2` alternate floating point type `mma` operation requires `sm_89` or higher.

`.e3m2`, `.e2m3` and `.e2m1` alternate floating point type `mma` operation requires `sm_120a`
and is supported on `sm_120f` from PTX ISA version 8.8.

Support for `.kind`, `.block_scale`, `.scale_vec_size` qualifier requires `sm_120a` and are
supported on `sm_120f` or higher in the same family from PTX ISA version 8.8.

Examples of half precision floating point type

```
// f16 elements in C and D matrix
.reg .f16x2 %Ra<2> %Rb<2> %Rc<4> %Rd<4>
mma.sync.aligned.m8n8k4.row.col.f16.f16.f16.f16
{%Rd0, %Rd1, %Rd2, %Rd3},
{%Ra0, %Ra1},
{%Rb0, %Rb1},
{%Rc0, %Rc1, %Rc2, %Rc3};

// f16 elements in C and f32 elements in D
.reg .f16x2 %Ra<2> %Rb<2> %Rc<4>
.reg .f32 %Rd<8>
mma.sync.aligned.m8n8k4.row.col.f32.f16.f16.f16
{%Rd0, %Rd1, %Rd2, %Rd3, %Rd4, %Rd5, %Rd6, %Rd7},
{%Ra0, %Ra1},
{%Rb0, %Rb1},
{%Rc0, %Rc1, %Rc2, %Rc3};

 // f32 elements in C and D
.reg .f16x2 %Ra<2>, %Rb<1>;
.reg .f32 %Rc<4>, %Rd<4>;
mma.sync.aligned.m16n8k8.row.col.f32.f16.f16.f32
  {%Rd0, %Rd1, %Rd2, %Rd3},
  {%Ra0, %Ra1},
  {%Rb0},
  {%Rc0, %Rc1, %Rc2, %Rc3};

.reg .f16x2 %Ra<4>, %Rb<2>, %Rc<2>, %Rd<2>;
mma.sync.aligned.m16n8k16.row.col.f16.f16.f16.f16
  {%Rd0, %Rd1},
  {%Ra0, %Ra1, %Ra2, %Ra3},
  {%Rb0, %Rb1},
  {%Rc0, %Rc1};

.reg .f16 %Ra<4>, %Rb<2>;
.reg .f32 %Rc<2>, %Rd<2>;
mma.sync.aligned.m16n8k16.row.col.f32.f16.f16.f32
  {%Rd0, %Rd1, %Rd2, %Rd3},
  {%Ra0, %Ra1, %Ra2, %Ra3},
  {%Rb0, %Rb1},
  {%Rc0, %Rc1, %Rc2, %Rc3};
```

  {%Rb},
  {%Rc0, %Rc1};

  {%Rd0, %Rd1},

  {%Rb},
  {%Rc0, %Rc1};

  {%Rd0, %Rd1, %Rd2, %Rd3},
  {%Ra0, %Ra1},
  {%Rb},

Examples of single bit type

```
// b1 elements in A and B
.reg .b32 %Ra, %Rb, %Rc<2>, %Rd<2>;
mma.sync.aligned.m8n8k128.row.col.s32.b1.b1.s32.and.popc
  {%Rd0, %Rd1},
  {%Ra},
  {%Rb},
  {%Rc0, %Rc1};

// b1 elements in A and B
.reg .b32 %Ra, %Rb, %Rc<2>, %Rd<2>;
mma.sync.aligned.m8n8k128.row.col.s32.b1.b1.s32.xor.popc
  {%Rd0, %Rd1},
  {%Ra},
  {%Rb},
  {%Rc0, %Rc1};

.reg .b32 %Ra<2>, %Rb, %Rc<4>, %Rd<4>;

##### 9.7.14.5.15. [Warp-level matrix load instruction: `ldmatrix`](#warp-level-matrix-instructions-ldmatrix)

`ldmatrix`

Collectively load one or more matrices from shared memory for `mma` instruction

Syntax

```
ldmatrix.sync.aligned.shape.num{.trans}{.ss}.type r, [p];

ldmatrix.sync.aligned.m8n16.num{.ss}.dst_fmt.src_fmt        r, [p];
ldmatrix.sync.aligned.m16n16.num.trans{.ss}.dst_fmt.src_fmt r, [p];

.shape   = {.m8n8, .m16n16};
.num     = {.x1, .x2, .x4};
.ss      = {.shared{::cta}};
.type    = {.b16, .b8};
.dst_fmt = { .b8x16 };
.src_fmt = { .b6x16_p32, .b4x16_p64 };
```

Description

Collectively load one or more matrices across all threads in a warp from the location indicated by
the address operand `p`, from `.shared` state space into destination register `r`. If no state
space is provided, generic addressing is used, such that the address in `p` points into
`.shared` space. If the generic address doesn’t fall in `.shared` state space, then the behavior
is undefined.

The `.shape` qualifier indicates the dimensions of the matrices being loaded. Each matrix element
holds 16-bit or 8-bit or 6-bit or 4-bit data.

Following table shows the matrix load case for each `.shape`.

| .shape | Matrix shape | Element size |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `.m8n8` | 8x8 | 16-bit |
| `.m16n16` | 16x16 | 8-bit or 6-bit or 4-bit |
| `.m8n16` | 8x16 | 6-bit or 4-bit |

Following table shows the valid use of 6-bit or 4-bit data load.

| .src_fmt | .shape | Source data | Padding | .dst_fmt |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `.b6x16_p32` | `.m8n16` | 16 6-bit elements | 32 bits | `.b8x16` (16 8-bit elements) |
| `.m16n16` |
| `.b4x16_p64` | `.m8n16` | 16 4-bit elements | 64 bits |
| `.m16n16` |

For `.b6x16_p32` format source data is 16 unsigned 6-bit elements with 32 bits padding.
For `.b4x16_p64` format source data is 16 unsigned 4-bit elements with 64 bits padding.

The values `.x1`, `.x2` and `.x4` for `.num` indicate one, two or four matrices
respectively. When `.shape` is `.m16n16`, only `.x1` and `.x2` are valid values for `.num`.

The mandatory `.sync` qualifier indicates that `ldmatrix` causes the executing thread to wait
until all threads in the warp execute the same `ldmatrix` instruction before resuming execution.

The mandatory `.aligned` qualifier indicates that all threads in the warp must execute the same
`ldmatrix` instruction. In conditionally executed code, an `ldmatrix` instruction should only be
used if it is known that all threads in the warp evaluate the condition identically, otherwise the
behavior is undefined.

The behavior of `ldmatrix` is undefined if all threads do not use the same qualifiers, or if any
thread in the warp has exited.

The destination operand `r` is a brace-enclosed vector expression consisting of 1, 2, or 4 32-bit
registers as per the value of `.num`. Each component of the vector expression holds a fragment
from the corresponding matrix.

Supported addressing modes for `p` are described in [Addresses as Operands](#addresses-as-operands).

Consecutive instances of row need not be stored contiguously in memory. The eight addresses required
for each matrix are provided by eight threads, depending upon the value of `.num` as shown in the
following table. Each address corresponds to the start of a matrix row. Addresses addr0–addr7
correspond to the rows of the first matrix, addresses addr8–addr15 correspond to the rows of the
second matrix, and so on.

| `.num` | Threads 0–7 | Threads 8–15 | Threads 16–23 | Threads 24–31 |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `.x1` | addr0–addr7 | – | – | – |
| `.x2` | addr0–addr7 | addr8–addr15 | – | – |
| `.x4` | addr0–addr7 | addr8–addr15 | addr16–addr23 | addr24–addr31 |

Note

For .target `sm_75` or below, all threads must contain valid addresses. Otherwise, the behavior
is undefined. For `.num = .x1` and `.num = .x2`, addresses contained in lower threads can be
copied to higher threads to achieve the expected behavior.

When reading 8x8 matrices, a group of four consecutive threads loads 16 bytes. The matrix addresses
must be naturally aligned accordingly.

Each thread in a warp loads fragments of a row, with thread 0 receiving the first fragment in its
register `r`, and so on. A group of four threads loads an entire row of the matrix as shown in
[Figure 104](#mma-ldmatrix-fragments).





[Figure: mma-ldmatrix-fragments]
LDMATRIX FRAGMENT LAYOUT: 8x8 Matrix with 16-bit elements

1. MATRIX DIMENSIONS AND TYPE
Matrix size: 8 rows x 8 columns
Element type: 16-bit elements (e.g., .f16 or .bf16)
This describes the ldmatrix instruction fragment layout for a single 8x8 matrix tile.

2. GRID STRUCTURE
Rows: 8 matrix rows (labeled 0 through 7)
Columns: 8 matrix columns (labeled 0 through 7)
Threads involved: T0 through T31 (32 threads total)
Register notation: Each cell shows "Tx:r" meaning thread Tx holds that element in register r

3. COMPLETE ELEMENT-TO-THREAD-REGISTER MAPPING

Row 0: Col0=T0:r, Col1=T0:r, Col2=T1:r, Col3=T1:r, Col4=T2:r, Col5=T2:r, Col6=T3:r, Col7=T3:r
Row 1: Col0=T4:r, Col1=T4:r, Col2=T5:r, Col3=T5:r, Col4=T6:r, Col5=T6:r, Col6=T7:r, Col7=T7:r
Row 2: Col0=T8:r, Col1=T8:r, Col2=T9:r, Col3=T9:r, Col4=T10:r, Col5=T10:r, Col6=T11:r, Col7=T11:r
Row 3: Col0=T12:r, Col1=T12:r, Col2=T13:r, Col3=T13:r, Col4=T14:r, Col5=T14:r, Col6=T15:r, Col7=T15:r
Row 4: Col0=T16:r, Col1=T16:r, Col2=T17:r, Col3=T17:r, Col4=T18:r, Col5=T18:r, Col6=T19:r, Col7=T19:r
Row 5: Col0=T20:r, Col1=T20:r, Col2=T21:r, Col3=T21:r, Col4=T22:r, Col5=T22:r, Col6=T23:r, Col7=T23:r
Row 6: Col0=T24:r, Col1=T24:r, Col2=T25:r, Col3=T25:r, Col4=T26:r, Col5=T26:r, Col6=T27:r, Col7=T27:r
Row 7: Col0=T28:r, Col1=T28:r, Col2=T29:r, Col3=T29:r, Col4=T30:r, Col5=T30:r, Col6=T31:r, Col7=T31:r

4. DATA TYPE
16-bit elements per cell. Two 16-bit elements are packed into a single 32-bit register r. Each thread holds exactly one 32-bit register containing two 16-bit values side by side.

5. GROUPING PATTERNS
Each matrix row is handled by exactly 4 consecutive threads:
Row 0: T0, T1, T2, T3
Row 1: T4, T5, T6, T7
Row 2: T8, T9, T10, T11
Row 3: T12, T13, T14, T15
Row 4: T16, T17, T18, T19
Row 5: T20, T21, T22, T23
Row 6: T24, T25, T26, T27
Row 7: T28, T29, T30, T31

Each thread covers exactly 2 consecutive columns within its row. Thread Tx covers columns (2*(Tx mod 4)) and (2*(Tx mod 4)+1) of its assigned row.

6. PACKING WITHIN REGISTERS
Each thread holds exactly 1 register (r) containing 2 packed 16-bit elements.
The two elements stored in a single thread's register are always from the same row and occupy two adjacent columns (e.g., T0 holds row0:col0 and row0:col1 packed into one 32-bit register).
The lower 16 bits of register r correspond to the even-numbered column, and the upper 16 bits correspond to the odd-numbered column.
Total storage: 32 threads x 1 register x 2 elements = 64 elements = 8x8 matrix, fully covered.

Total storage: 32 threads x 1 register x 2 elements = 64 elements = 8x8 matrix, fully covered.


Figure 104 ldmatrix fragment layout for one 8x8 Matrix with 16-bit elements

When `.num` = `.x2`, the elements of the second matrix are loaded in the next destination
register in each thread as per the layout in above table. Similarly, when `.num` = `.x4`,
elements of the third and fourth matrices are loaded in the subsequent destination registers in each
thread.

For matrix shape 16x16, two destination registers `r0` and `r1` of type `.b32` must be
specified and in each register four 8-bit elements are loaded. For 4-bit or 6-bit data, 8-bit
element will have 4 bits or 2 bits of padding respectively.
Refer [Optional Decompression](#tcgen05-optional-decompression) for more details
on these formats.

An entire row of the matrix can be loaded by a group of four consecutive and aligned threads.
Each thread in a warp loads 4 consecutive columns across 2 rows as shown in the
[Figure 105](#mma-ldmatrix-fragments-1616).





[Figure: mma-ldmatrix-fragments-1616]
LDMATRIX FRAGMENT LAYOUT FOR ONE 16x16 MATRIX WITH 8-BIT ELEMENTS

=============================================================
OVERVIEW
=============================================================
Matrix dimensions: 16 rows x 16 columns
Element data type: 8-bit elements
Total threads involved: T0 through T31 (32 threads)
Registers per thread: 2 registers (r0 and r1)
Matrix identity: This is a single 16x16 matrix loaded via ldmatrix

=============================================================
GRID STRUCTURE
=============================================================
The table has:
  Rows (labeled Row\Col): 0 through 15 (16 matrix rows)
  Columns (labeled 0 through 15): 16 matrix columns

Each cell contains an entry of the form "Tx:ry" where:
  Tx = thread number (T0 through T31)
  ry = register index (r0 or r1)

=============================================================
REGISTER PACKING
=============================================================
Each register (r0 or r1) is a 32-bit register.
Since elements are 8-bit, each 32-bit register holds 4 elements.
Each thread holds 2 registers (r0 and r1), covering 8 elements total.
32 threads x 8 elements = 256 elements = 16x16 matrix (confirmed).

Within each register, 4 consecutive columns of a given row are packed.
Specifically, for a given thread Tx:
  r0 holds the 4 elements from columns [c, c+1, c+2, c+3] of one row
  r1 holds the 4 elements from columns [c, c+1, c+2, c+3] of another row

LDMATRIX FRAGMENT LAYOUT FOR ONE 16x16 MATRIX WITH 8-BIT ELEMENTS

=============================================================
OVERVIEW
=============================================================
Matrix dimensions: 16 rows x 16 columns
Element data type: 8-bit elements
Total threads involved: T0 through T31 (32 threads)
Registers per thread: 2 registers (r0 and r1)
Matrix identity: This is a single 16x16 matrix loaded via ldmatrix

=============================================================
GRID STRUCTURE
=============================================================
The table has:
  Rows (labeled Row\Col): 0 through 15 (16 matrix rows)
  Columns (labeled 0 through 15): 16 matrix columns

Each cell contains an entry of the form "Tx:ry" where:
  Tx = thread number (T0 through T31)
  ry = register index (r0 or r1)

=============================================================
REGISTER PACKING
=============================================================
Each register (r0 or r1) is a 32-bit register.
Since elements are 8-bit, each 32-bit register holds 4 elements.
Each thread holds 2 registers (r0 and r1), covering 8 elements total.
32 threads x 8 elements = 256 elements = 16x16 matrix (confirmed).

Within each register, 4 consecutive columns of a given row are packed.
Specifically, for a given thread Tx:
  r0 holds the 4 elements from columns [c, c+1, c+2, c+3] of one row
  r1 holds the 4 elements from columns [c, c+1, c+2, c+3] of another row

=============================================================
COMPLETE CELL-BY-CELL LAYOUT TABLE

Figure 105 ldmatrix fragment layout for one 16x16 matrix with 8-bit elements

For matrix shape 8x16, one destination register `r0` of type `.b32` must be specified where four
8-bit elements are loaded in the register. For 4-bit or 6-bit data, 8-bit element will have 4 bits
or 2 bits of padding respectively.

An entire row of the matrix can be loaded by a group of four consecutive and aligned threads.
Each thread in a warp loads 4 consecutive columns as shown in [Figure 106](#mma-ldmatrix-fragments-816).

- Each thread holds exactly 4 consecutive columns within a single row, all packed into register r0
- 4 threads cover each full row (16 columns / 4 columns per thread = 4 threads per row)

  Row 0: T0 (cols 0-3), T1 (cols 4-7), T2 (cols 8-11), T3 (cols 12-15)
  Row 1: T4 (cols 0-3), T5 (cols 4-7), T6 (cols 8-11), T7 (cols 12-15)
  Row 2: T8 (cols 0-3), T9 (cols 4-7), T10 (cols 8-11), T11 (cols 12-15)
  Row 3: T12 (cols 0-3), T13 (cols 4-7), T14 (cols 8-11), T15 (cols 12-15)

  Row 5: T20 (cols 0-3), T21 (cols 4-7), T22 (cols 8-11), T23 (cols 12-15)
  Row 6: T24 (cols 0-3), T25 (cols 4-7), T26 (cols 8-11), T27 (cols 12-15)
  Row 7: T28 (cols 0-3), T29 (cols 4-7), T30 (cols 8-11), T31 (cols 12-15)

PACKING DETAILS:
- Each thread holds 4 eight-bit elements packed into one 32-bit register (r0)
- The 4 elements correspond to 4 consecutive columns within the thread's assigned row
- Each 8-bit element itself encodes either a 4-bit or 6-bit sub-value (as indicated by the figure caption)
- No additional registers (r1, r2, etc.) are used; the entire fragment fits in a single register per thread
